findings.evidence: `rg` hit: `mathlib/Mathlib/Algebra/BigOperators/Pi.lean:100: lemma prod_indicator_const (s : Finset ι) (f : ι → Set κ) (g : κ → R) : ∏ i ∈ s, (f i).indicator g = (⋂ x ∈ s, f x).indicator (g ^ #s)`; line 70 proves the same finite intersection-indicator identity for the sets `{ω | X (k i) ω ∈ C i}`.
findings.fix: Rewrite this lemma as a thin specialization of `prod_indicator_const`/`prod_indicator_apply` with `S i = {ω | X (k i) ω ∈ C i}`, then identify `⋂ i ∈ Finset.univ, S i` with `blockCylinder X k C`.
findings.issue: `blockIndicatorProd_eq_indicator` is the coordinate-cylinder specialization of Mathlib's finite product-of-indicators lemma, but reproves it by a manual `prod_eq_one`/`prod_eq_zero` split.

summary: One indicator-product lemma reimplements an existing Mathlib API instead of specializing it.
